content: tcpConnectOnce(host, port, timeoutMs) in src/preview/launch.ts (warren-49d9 / pl-592f step 1) is the phase-1 TCP-only probe. Uses Bun.connect with an open/error/timeout settle pattern; resolves 'connected' the instant the kernel completes the TCP handshake (immediately closes the socket via sock.end()) or 'not_connected' on refused/unreachable/timeout. Phase-2 readiness keeps using the HTTP probeOnce; phase-1 was misclassifying slow-first-headers dev servers (Next.js mid-compile) as not_connected and burning the connect budget. Exported (not file-local) so step-3 unit tests can drive it directly without spinning up attemptPreviewLaunch, and so noUnusedLocals stays happy during the multi-step plan rollout (step 2 wires it into the phase-1 loop).

launchPreview phase-1 (warren-44ed / pl-592f step 2) calls tcpConnectImpl = input.tcpConnect ?? tcpConnectOnce on '127.0.0.1':port — raw TCP handshake, not HTTP fetch. probeOnce stays in phase-2 only. Tests inject tcpConnect via the new optional LaunchPreviewInput.tcpConnect field; helpers alwaysConnected/alwaysRefused/fakeTcp in launch.test.ts cover the common test shapes. Real callers leave tcpConnect undefined so the real Bun.connect-based tcpConnectOnce runs.

tcpConnectOnce unit tests (src/preview/launch.test.ts, warren-f04c / pl-592f step 3) cover the real Bun.connect path on localhost: 'connected' against a Bun.listen server (asserts socket closes), 'not_connected' against a refused port (listener stopped first), and 'not_connected' against 192.0.2.1:1 (TEST-NET-1, dropped SYN) under timeoutMs. The slow-first-headers regression test in the two-phase describe block injects tcpConnect=[connected] + a fetch that throws DOMException AbortError on the first 2 calls then returns 200 — proves the warren-c3a2 bug stays fixed (phase-1 transitions immediately, phase-2 readiness budget absorbs the mid-headers aborts).
